feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Column | I’m a gastroenterologist. Here’s what antibiotics really do to your gut.
entry.summary: Skip the probiotic with your antibiotics, and bolster your microbiome with these three strategies instead.

entry.title: Why your next 'miracle supplement' should be a jar of raw honey
entry.summary: One of the most powerful health boosters isn't found at your local pharmacy or through a high-end website. It comes straight from nature: high-quality, raw honey.

entry.title: Sheriff’s office seeks help identifying two people in Grand Traverse County
entry.summary: GRAND TRAVERSE COUNTY, Mich., (WPBN/WGTU) -- The Grand Traverse Sheriff's Office is asking for help in identifying two people.If you recognize them, contact the

entry.title: Graphene quantum dots show promise in targeting Parkinson's-related protein clumping
entry.summary: The buildup of a protein called 𝛂-synuclein (ASN) into toxic clumps is a hallmark of synucleinopathies, a group of neurodegenerative diseases that includes Parkinson's and multiple system atrophy (MSA). These aggregates are associated with cellular dysfuncti…

entry.title: Most Covid-19 cases linked to mild variant
entry.summary: The NB.1.8.1 strain has become the dominant Covid-19 variant in Thailand over the past year, although health authorities say there is no evidence it spreads faster or causes more severe illness.

entry.title: ‘Stomach flu’ outbreak slams hikers on California section of Pacific Crest Trail
entry.summary: A norovirus outbreak in the Wrightwood area of the Pacific Crest Trail in San Bernardino County has sickened dozens of hikers; public health urges strict hygiene.

entry.title: US well-positioned to deal with current Ebola outbreak, Deborah Birx says
entry.summary: She said the country has "a deep bench" even in federal agencies without a confirmed leader.

entry.title: The 5 Best Protein-Rich Canned Foods, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Want to add more protein to your diet? Stock up on these dietitian-recommended high protein pantry staples like canned trout, chicken and chili.

entry.title: Suspected Ebola cases in eastern DR Congo top 900 as health workers struggle with aid cuts
entry.summary: As the number of suspected Ebola cases in the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo passed 900 over the weekend, health workers said that the chaos left by years of fighting and local government failures has been compounded by sweeping cuts to international ai…

entry.title: What to know about sepsis, the dangerous condition that contributed to NASCAR driver Kyle Busch’s death
entry.summary: Kyle Busch’s family says the NASCAR superstar died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis, a dangerous condition that many people do not understand.

entry.title: Experts have found a new type of cholesterol. And it could be the most dangerous
entry.summary: Experts have found a new type of cholesterol, and it could be the most dangerous for your long-term health.

entry.title: Be Ready for Camp
entry.summary: From our popular camp packing lists, to our best tips, you’ll feel like a pro as you get your child ready for sleepaway camp. Whether it’s your first time sending a child to sleepaway camp, or you’re already a veteran, you still need a list. For years, BCP Re…

entry.title: Fatherhood Dramatically Rewires Your Brain, Scans Reveal
entry.summary: The female brain undergoes profound changes to prepare for parenthood.

entry.title: The High-Protein Breakfast Jackie Kennedy Had Almost Every Morning
entry.summary: Discover the high-protein breakfast Jackie Kennedy enjoyed nearly every day, featuring eggs, bacon, honey, orange juice and milk for a healthy start.

entry.title: The Remarkable Proliferation of Cancer Immunotherapies
entry.summary: On a Path to Interception—Prevention Vaccines

entry.title: DR Congo Ebola cases rise amid distrust, armed conflict zone
entry.summary: Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.

entry.title: Transcript: Dr. Deborah Birx on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
entry.summary: The following is the transcript of the interview with Dr. Deborah Birx that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 24, 2026.

entry.title: Stress Incontinence? Women Swear This Trick Stops Bladder Leaks Instantly
entry.summary: You laugh at your best friend's joke—and feel a little leak. You sneeze in the grocery store and freeze. You skip the trampoline at your granddaughter's birt...

entry.title: Florida is becoming a hotbed for reversing, or at least slowing down, biological age
entry.summary: Florida is now considered a hotbed for clinics, treatments, trials and supplements.

entry.title: Five Seconds Into Sex, Something Aggravating Happens to My Wife. I’m Sick of It.
entry.summary: We shouldn't have to live like this.

entry.title: Americans exposed to hantavirus on ship enjoy Nebraska hospitality while in quarantine
entry.summary: The 18 American passengers who were exposed to hantavirus on the MV Hondius cruise ship are getting a taste of Nebraska hospitality as they wait to find out how much longer they will have to remain in quarantine at the hospital in Omaha.

entry.title: Drug Used to Treat Asthma and Allergies May Also Help Fight Aggressive Cancers
entry.summary: A drug often used to treat asthma and allergies may also fight aggressive cancers, it’s already approved by the FDA.

entry.title: Pennsylvania schools shared widely debunked vaccine claims with thousands of parents
entry.summary: False

entry.title: Woman Says Doctors Ignored “Canker Sore” Until Tongue Cancer Nearly Took Her Life
entry.summary: “The medics told me I was a miracle,” a 42-year-old nurse from Florida revisited how a red sore on her tongue led to a near-fatal experience.

entry.title: Ebola Virus Outbreak: AP Govt On High Alert!
entry.summary: It wasn’t long before the entire world suffered the consequences of Covid outbreak, which changed the entire living systems and societal structure. Ever since, there have been multiple cases of other virus outbreaks that have been threatening the social order…

entry.title: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring
entry.summary: The number of cases — and deaths — in Bangladesh is staggering. As of Sunday, 528 have died, mostly children. How did this measles outbreak begin? And how is the country responding?

entry.title: Oncologists Wish You'd Stop Doing These 4 Things
entry.summary: Oncologists and cancer prevention experts explain how lifestyle impacts cancer risk, and the diet, exercise, and emotional habits that make a difference.

entry.title: Thailand urges calm over S’pore’s Covid-19 spike; no severe outbreak from dominant NB.1.8.1 strain
entry.summary: Reported cases in Thailand have risen over the past month, but the number remains below the five-year median. Read more at straitstimes.com. Read more at straitstimes.com.

entry.title: Younger adults are having strokes. Here’s how to lower your risk
entry.summary: Health officials note greater prevalence of adults 18 to 55 with strokes, emphasizing the importance of recognizing warning signs and acting quickly.

entry.title: Surprising research reveals why you shouldn't add bananas to your smoothies
entry.summary: Researchers found that adding bananas to berry smoothies can dramatically reduce the body’s ability to absorb healthy flavanols. The surprising discovery shows that even simple food combinations can change how much nutrition your body actually gets.

entry.title: How a distinct communication subspace in the brain turns goals into actions
entry.summary: Humans continuously adapt their actions and behaviors in response to changes in their surrounding environment. Past neuroscience studies suggest that this adaptation process relies on the brain's ability to translate abstract goals or rules into specific phys…

entry.title: I'm A Hip Surgeon. Here's The 1 Thing I'd Never Do If I Have Hip Pain.
entry.summary: This behavior can lead to major problems down the road.

entry.title: Red Cross volunteers die from suspected Ebola in DR Congo
entry.summary: They are thought to have caught the virus before the outbreak was identified, the Red Cross says.

entry.title: Anti-vaxxers get a chance to fall in love with others who do not understand science (Opinion)
entry.summary: The organization initially invited anti-vaxxers to “find love with mRNA free partners.” Pickings were poor since all living creatures naturally have mRNA in their cells.

entry.title: Doctors Reveal The Most Common Side Effects Of GLP-1s — And How To Manage Them
entry.summary: Ozempic, Wegovy and other GLP-1 medications are popular but can cause unwanted health concerns.

entry.title: 6 Things to Do in the Morning to Help Prevent Inflammation Throughout the Day
entry.summary: Easy morning habits like drinking coffee or tea, eating berries, gentle movement, mindfulness and getting outside may all help fight inflammation.

entry.title: 6 rashes and infections you can get from the beach
entry.summary: There are a handful of bacteria, viruses and even parasites that can live in sand and salt water. Learn how to minimize your chances of bringing them home.

entry.title: Why Garlic Repels Mosquitoes and Keeps Them From Breeding
entry.summary: Garlic, as your grandmother may have told you, repels mosquitoes; it also completely blocks them from mating and laying eggs. Diallyl disulfide, it turns out, deserves the credit.

entry.title: Scientists discover hidden driver of aging — Simple supplement reversed brain decline
entry.summary: A newly identified brain protein may play a major role in how the body ages. Researchers discovered that declining levels of Menin in the hypothalamus triggered inflammation, memory problems, bone loss, and other aging-related changes in mice. Restoring Menin…

entry.title: Simple Fiber Supplement Cuts Knee Arthritis Pain in Just 6 Weeks, Study Finds
entry.summary: A daily inulin supplement may help reduce knee osteoarthritis pain while revealing a possible link between gut health, muscle function, and pain sensitivity. For millions of people living with knee osteoarthritis, managing chronic pain often means choosing be…

entry.title: Locals near epicenter of the Ebola crisis express fear and concerns about misinformation
entry.summary: Hélène Akilimali says she’s taking every precaution against contracting Ebola, including always wearing a face mask in public. But her work as a cocoa seller in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) - the epicenter of the latest outbreak - brings her int…

entry.title: Your blood pressure may look normal today, but these silent changes could be warning you about future hypertension
entry.summary: Most people think high blood pressure begins the day a doctor finally says the word “hypertension.” But the truth is often quieter than that. The body usually starts sending signals much earlier. Not dramatic alarms. Just small shifts that slowly become patte…

entry.title: Your Blood Type Affects Risk of Early Stroke, Study Reveals
entry.summary: You might not think your blood type has much to do with your health, but research suggests that it can have an effect.

entry.title: Stray cat tests positive for rabies after biting man
entry.summary: A stray cat in Burrillville has tested positive for rabies after biting a person in the area of Whipple Road, according to the Rhode Island Department of Health

entry.title: 3 Red Cross volunteers die of Ebola as number of cases rises in Uganda
entry.summary: Three Red Cross volunteers have died, believed to have contracted the Ebola virus during a humanitarian mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo in March, the organization said.“At the time of the intervention, the community was not aware of the Ebola …

entry.title: Common weedkiller linked to the development of superbugs in hospitals
entry.summary: Scientists have discovered that glyphosate weedkiller could contribute to the development of drug-resistant bacteria outside of hospitals.

entry.title: We Were Wrong About Fasting, Massive Study Finds
entry.summary: As effective as fasting can be for weight loss, it's often thought that depriving the body of sustenance might have a negative impact on brainpower.

entry.title: Being asked to help dampens the joy of doing good, according to children in multiple countries
entry.summary: A cross-cultural study reveals that children often believe people are less willing and happy to help others when instructed to do so. However, the strength of this belief varies across different cultural and socioeconomic backgrounds.

entry.title: The 5 Best Foods to Eat After 5 P.M. for Better Blood Sugar
entry.summary: Discover the best foods to eat after 5 p.m. for better blood sugar control, including expert tips and dietitian-approved dinner ideas.

entry.title: What to know about sepsis: Life-threatening but hard to spot
entry.summary: Consider sepsis an invisible threat. It kills about 350,000 Americans a year and may cause nearly one-fifth of deaths globally. But you won’t find it on any “leading causes of death” lists.
